subject: Human Resources
title: Religious Accommodation
description: A quick win: understand Religious Accommodation.

hook.text: An employee asks to leave early on Fridays for religious observance. The manager says no. The law says: try harder.

buildup.text: Title VII requires employers to reasonably accommodate employees' sincerely held religious beliefs and practices.

discovery.text: Accommodations include schedule changes, dress code exceptions, prayer breaks, and holiday swaps with coworkers.

twist.text: In 2023, the Supreme Court raised the bar—employers must show accommodation causes 'substantial' hardship, not just minor.

climax.text: Balancing religious freedom with business needs requires good-faith dialogue—not blanket denials or assumptions.

punchline.text: Accommodation starts with a conversation, not a policy.

quiz.question: What standard must employers meet to deny religious accommodation?
options: Show the accommodation causes substantial hardship
